_comment: SAMPLE (sense-check only). Part 1: test cases as in tests/cases/. Part 2: golden set as in tests/golden/ — human-labeled judge-check examples; the judge must agree with 'label' on >=90% before its verdicts count.

cases.description: vague exploratory ask; correct read is upper-left
turns: My app feels slow sometimes and I think caching might help but I'm not sure where the problem is. What should I do?
cases.expected_shape: positioning statement naming upper-left; <assumptions> block with request-specific content; >=2 options; recommendation + why; exact next prompt

cases.description: fully specified ask; correct read is bottom-right
turns: Write a git commit message for this diff: renamed `getUser` to `fetchUser` in api.js and updated its 3 call sites. Conventional Commits format, type refactor, no body.
cases.expected_shape: one commit message, no alternatives, near-zero commentary

cases.description: single-fact lookup; framework must NOT trigger
turns: What's the default port for PostgreSQL?
cases.expected_shape: trivial marker with reason, direct answer, no positioning statement, no assumptions block, no options

golden.response_excerpt: **Operating from: Upper-Left (Plan + Verify)** — lots of unknowns about where the slowness is, so I'm exploring before recommending...
golden.label: pass

golden.response_excerpt: **Operating from: Bottom-Right (Build + Quality)** — here's a Redis caching layer for your app...
golden.label: fail
golden.why: claims bottom-right on a high-uncertainty ask; wrong quadrant read

golden.response_excerpt: <assumptions><project>I assume your codebase follows best practices.</project><task>I assume you want a good solution.</task></assumptions>
golden.label: fail
golden.why: hollow compliance — block present (2a passes) but content is filler that fits any request; 2b exists to catch exactly this

golden.response_excerpt:
refactor: rename getUser to fetchUser

Alternatively, if you prefer more detail, you could also use: refactor(api): rename getUser to fetchUser across call sites
golden.label: fail
golden.why: right-side case; offered an unrequested alternative

golden.response_excerpt: **Trivial — matrix not applied.** Single-fact lookup; the answer doesn't depend on any assumption about your project. PostgreSQL's default port is 5432.
golden.label: pass
golden.why: names the classification and gives a reason specific to this request

golden.response_excerpt: **Trivial — matrix not applied.** This is simple. PostgreSQL's default port is 5432.
golden.label: fail
golden.why: reason is boilerplate that would fit any request — fails the swap test
